articles.title: Punjab Weather Update: Punjab Braces for High Heat and Peak Power Demand - Rozana Spokesman
articles.full_text:
Punjab is currently experiencing a challenging weather pattern as the monsoon remains sluggish, leading to soaring humidity and uncomfortable heat across the state. While residents seek relief from the sweltering conditions, the India Meteorological Department has indicated that hot and humid weather will persist through July 17.
The state’s meteorological outlook suggests a temporary lull in rainfall activity, as the essential atmospheric factors required to invigorate the monsoon, such as the southward shift of the monsoon trough, are currently absent. However, a new Western Disturbance is expected to influence the region starting July 19, which meteorologists anticipate will reignite rainfall.
In this climate, the Punjab State Power Corporation Limited has faced unprecedented pressure on its electrical infrastructure. On July 14, the state successfully met a record-breaking peak power demand of 17,328 MW. Despite these soaring requirements for agricultural and domestic use, officials confirmed that no power cuts were imposed, reflecting a stable supply management.
Reservoir management remains a priority for the Bhakra Beas Management Board during this period. Data indicates that water levels in key dams are being closely monitored to balance irrigation needs and flood control, ensuring that the state remains prepared for potential heavy inflows that may accompany the expected late-July rainfall.
As Punjab navigates this humid spell, authorities have advised citizens to stay cautious during upcoming thunderstorm activities. With the anticipated shift in weather patterns later this week, the state hopes for a much-needed cooling effect to ease both the environmental heat and the relentless pressure on its power grid.

articles.title: ਵਾਹਨ ਚਾਲਕਾਂ ਲਈ ਵੱਡਾ ਅਲਰਟ, ਦੁਪਹਿਰ 3 ਵਜੇ ਤੱਕ ਇਨ੍ਹਾਂ ਰੂਟਾਂ ''ਤੇ ਨਾ ਕਰੋ ਸਫ਼ਰ, ਪੜ੍ਹੋ ਐਡਵਾਈਜ਼ਰੀ - Jagbani

articles.full_text:
ਵਾਹਨ ਚਾਲਕਾਂ ਲਈ ਵੱਡਾ ਅਲਰਟ, ਦੁਪਹਿਰ 3 ਵਜੇ ਤੱਕ ਇਨ੍ਹਾਂ ਰੂਟਾਂ 'ਤੇ ਨਾ ਕਰੋ ਸਫ਼ਰ, ਪੜ੍ਹੋ ਐਡਵਾਈਜ਼ਰੀ
